qa.user_verbatim_complaint: /redev User needs not satisfied: Codex suggested adding a positive regression test for the CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback path — explicitly deferred per BA spec Won't Have.
qa.overall_assessment: Both acceptance criteria satisfied. The new test method test_succeeds_with_only_claude_code_session_id exists, passes individually (AC1), and the full target test file passes with 16 tests (AC2). The test correctly strips both SID vars and injects only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, with TemporaryDirectory outer and patch.dict inner nesting. Task-owned diff is limited to hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py.

success_criteria_results.criterion: The targeted test passes when run individually
Full target test file passes with 16 tests, 0 failed
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest output: 16 passing tests listed, all PASSED, 16 passed in 0.04s. Count increased from 15 (prior cycle) to 16 (this cycle) as expected.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The test exercises the specific fallback branch: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID absent -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID used. The _resolve_sid() function at write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py:56 uses or-based evaluation; stripping CLAUDE_SESSION_ID and setting only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID forces the fallback. The test passes, proving the branch works correctly.

file_scope_verification.git_diff_evidence: git diff HEAD -- hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py confirms single 13-line insertion; no deletions. Broader working tree is dirty with pre-existing uncommitted changes per baseline_dirty_snapshot in dev-report, but those are not task-owned.

build_verification.skip_reason: Change is a pure test file addition with no deployed code. No build or service restart required.

regression_test_results.details: All 16 tests in hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py passed. Pre-existing 15 tests unchanged.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: AC artifact check.cmd uses 'python -m pytest ...' but 'python' is not on PATH on this system (only python3 is available). The dispatch AC commands used 'pytest ...' / 'python3 -m pytest ...' which are runnable and passed. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift in the acceptance-criteria JSON artifact; the dispatch instructions and BA ticket use the correct runnable form. Not blocking for this cycle since dispatch ACs are unambiguous and were verified.

Finding 4 is OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX; logged in codex_observation_only array.
